tossups.question: The first white person to receive the death penalty in this country, Mariette Bosch, was nicknamed this country’s “White Mischief” for murdering her husband’s lover. In 1988, an Angolan military jet mistakenly shot a plane containing this country’s president Quett Masire (“mah-SEE-ray”). The Malan (“ma-LAHN”) government threatened to cut off uranium supplies to the UK in an unsuccessful attempt to block the interracial marriage of Englishwoman Ruth Williams to this country’s first president, Sir Seretse (“seh-RET-say”) Khama. This country’s Orapa diamond mine, opened in 1971, is now the largest in the world. This country has been the oldest continuous multiparty democracy in Africa since receiving independence as Bechuanaland. For 10 points, what country led from Gaborone (“gab-oh-RO-nee”) is directly north of South Africa?

tossups.question: Rates for associative substitution of this compound in metal aquo complexes gave evidence for a pre-equilibrium step in the Eigen–Wilkins mechanism. Ligands of this compound coordinated to two pyridine-bridged ruthenium centers form a classic mixed-valence complex isolated by Carol Creutz and Henry Taube. This compound causes mercury(I) (“mercury-one”) chloride to disproportionate into a black solution, hence the name “calomel.” Planctomycetota bacteria anaerobically oxidize this compound into hydrazine, which can also be done by the Olin–Raschig process. The first maser was built with this compound due to its low barrier for umbrella inversion. This is the lightest trigonal pyramidal compound. For 10 points, the Haber–Bosch process generates what compound with formula NH3?

tossups.question: In a Russian folktale, a person of this type befriends a magic pike and rides a stove to see the king. In a reimagining of a folk character similar to Emelya, little devils teach this type of person how to make soldiers from straw and gold from oak leaves. That character of this type also inspired a Cesar Cui opera, in which he uses pots meant for his family to feed hungry birds and marries the Tsar’s daughter. In one story, Simon the Warrior and Tarras the Pot-Bellied are brothers of (*) Ivan-durak, who exemplifies the folkloric archetype of this kind of person. A group of people of this type who worship Saint Grobian journey to Narragonia in a book by Sebastian Brant. One painting depicts a nun and a monk playing a lute inside a vessel with a tree for a mast bearing the title people of this type. For 10 points, Hieronymous Bosch painted an allegorical ship run by what title kind of people?

tossups.question: Several people in this painting share bites of a blueberry-like fruit while swimming in a pool. This painting, which depicts a grisaille (“griz-EYE”) version of the world’s creation when closed, includes an elephant and giraffe among two nudes. The face of this painting’s artist appears on a “treeman” made out of a cracked egg. An ear between two knives and a demon defecating people through an hourglass appear in a dark section of this painting with the background of a burning city. Dozens of nudes frolicking among this 1500 painting’s title scene contrast with its rightmost Hell panel. For 10 points, name this triptych by Hieronymous Bosch.

tossups.question: This mountain’s south-facing slopes contain a series of sandstone caves known as the Wynberg Caves. The eastern slopes of this mountain support the Newlands Forest, which contains an endemic red orchid known as the “pride of” this mountain as well as a namesake ghost frog. This mountain is accessible via Skeleton Gorge from Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, while a more central route which splits through the cliffs of this mountain is the Platteklip Gorge. The Twelve Apostles are a series of lesser summits on the west side of this mountain. According to the Lusiads, this peak’s shape was inherited from the fallen titan (*) Adamastor, who is equated with the Korana chief T’kana according to a novel of Andre Brink. This peak was notably summitted by an explorer who carved a cross into the adjacent Lion’s Head on the way to its summit. That summit is now marked by a cairn known as Maclear’s Beacon. The only constellation named for a terrestrial geographic feature was inspired by a layer of fog on this mountain observed by Nicholas Louis de la Caille. When this mountain is not covered by orographic clouds, it is visible from beaches like the Bloubergstrand, as well as offshore Robben Island. FTP, name this iconic mountain which stands above Cape Town.

tossups.question: A character in this novel gets the idea for a grand project after listening to a black-clad specter lecture on holy fornication and poverty inspired by the Biblical Adam while in a cathedral in a besieged Flemish city. A section of this novel narrated in the second person relates the story of a youth shipwrecked on a black sand beach who is encountered by a procession including whispering kabbalists and a sumptuously dressed female dwarf. A 90-year-old concierge in this novel gives birth to a baby with six toes on each foot and a wine-red cross on his back with the help of a sandwich board wearer who dreams of the phrase “Imagine, the first animal that dreamt of another animal.” This novel, whose expansive dramatis personae includes Don Quixote and Hieronymus Bosch, opens in a future (*) Paris where the Arc de Triomphe is made of sand and the Eiffel Tower is a zoo. In this 800-page novel divided into the sections Old World, New World, and Next World, characters like the student Ludovico and the peasant girl and witch Celestina are reborn in different historical eras after first appearing during Phillip II’s building of El Escorial. For 10 points, name this epic novel by Carlos Fuentes, whose title means “our earth.”

This element is used as the catalyst in the high-temperature version of the Fischer-Tropsch process. It’s not aluminum, but this element’s chloride can be used as a catalyst to electrophilically halogenate (“HAL-uh-jin-ate”) aromatic rings in the Friedel-Crafts reactions. This metal is bound to two (*) cyclopentadienyl (“cyclo-penta-dye-EE-nil”) rings in the earliest discovered sandwich compound. A catalyst of this metal is used to produce ammonia in the Haber-Bosch (“HAH-ber BOSH”) process. For 10 points, name this metal found alongside carbon in steel.

tossups.question: This figure is a pale, old, three-headed man in white billowing robes in a painting by Leonora Carrington inspired by an earlier work in which this man sits by a river with a pig at his feet. While speaking to this man, a hermit gazes up at a raven who brings two loaves of bread in a painting at the Unterlinden (“OONT-er-lin-den”) Museum. Michelangelo’s earliest known work copies an engraving titled for this man by Martin Schongauer (“SHONE-gao-er”). This man is whisked away into the sky above sick people crossing a bridge at the left of a triptych by Hieronymus Bosch. An Alsatian (“al-SAY-shin”) monastery whose brothers cured ergotism commissioned an altarpiece in which this man is attacked by bird-headed demons. For 10 points, the Isenheim Altarpiece depicts that attack on what church father who was tempted in the Egyptian desert?
tossups.answer: Saint Anthony [accept The Temptation of St. Anthony] (The earlier painting is Bosch’s Temptation of Saint Anthony.)

tossups.question: Shrunken heads and skulls hang from trees within the inner chambers of this structure. A work within this structure includes depictions of the 9/11 attacks and a post-meteor-impact Earth, which bears a striking resemblance to Hieronymous Bosch’s work. This structure includes a “Bridge of Rebirth” that rises above a sea of outstretched human hands, made of stone. The entrance to this structure, its “Gate of Heaven,” is guarded by two statues representing (*) Death and Rahu. A mural in this structure features various characters, such as Neo from The Matrix and Doraemon, which serve as symbols of modernity that one must escape from to achieve purity. This structure was redesigned by Chalermchai Kositpipat, built in his native Chiang Rai province. For 10 points, name this temple located in Pa O Don Chai, notable for its surreal imagery and references to modern culture in its decorations.

Michelangelo’s oldest painting adds a halo to this figure as part of a full-color copy of an engraving by Martin Schongauer. This figure comprises the right panel of the closed Isenheim Altarpiece, which was created for a monastery dedicated to him that treated skin disease. tossups.question: This nucleophilic (“NOO-klee-oh-FILL-ick”) compound attacks the carbon of an aldehyde in the first part of the Strecker synthesis. Due to solvated electrons, solutions of alkali metals in this compound produce a deep blue colour. Two of these molecules react with hydrogen peroxide to produce water and the rocket fuel hydrazine. For 10 points, name this molecule that contains three nitrogen-hydrogen bonds.

tossups.question: A mixture of deionized water and this compound makes up commercial diesel exhaust fluid. A side product of the Bosch–Meiser process for producing this compound is biuret (“bye-yur-ET”), which is toxic to plants when used in fertilizers like UAN. Radiolabeled variants of this compound can detect Helicobacter pylori, which hydrolyzes it to increase stomach pH. Variations of this compound can be synthesized by reacting phosgene with primary amines. Ornithine and this compound are generated from ammonia in the body in this compound’s namesake cycle. Friedrich Wöhler disproved vitalism by making this compound the first organic compound to be synthesized from inorganic starting materials. For 10 points, name this nitrogen-containing compound excreted in urine.
